aesthetic.direction: Minimalist, Apple Human Interface Guidelines inspired
aesthetic.mood: Warm spring daylight — soft, calm, unobtrusive
aesthetic.paletteNotes: Cream canvas, peach and sage accents; avoid harsh saturated primaries

typography.fontStack: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif

visual.implementationNote: web/src/styles/tokens.css mirrors these tokens as CSS custom properties.

app.name: Zoho metadata exporter
app.shortDescription: Paste a shared Zoho browser session, validate once via CRM, then choose which products and data types to export. Receives one downloadable ZIP per system.

assumptions.credentials: One session (Cookie, CSRF, org) is valid across Zoho CRM, Books, and future apps.

validationStrategy.description: Validate session runs a CRM-only smoke test. On success, the same session is treated as valid for every other Zoho app in the UI without separate per-app checks.
validationStrategy.implementationNote: Backend intent matches existing crm/testAuth.js; Books-specific probes are not required to gate export.

assumptions.downloadPackaging: One archive per system. All selected export types under that system are bundled into that system's single ZIP.
assumptions.scriptPaths: Known only to the backend job registry; never shown in the client UI.

split.responsiveBehavior: Stack vertically on narrow viewports: left panel (session) first, then right panel (export selection).

regions.title: Session
regions.purpose: Enter shared Zoho session data and validate once.

regions.title: What to download
regions.purpose: Choose systems and data types; download yields one ZIP per system that has exportable selections.

routes.label: Settings
routes.purpose: Customize ZIP file naming patterns per system and other non-secret preferences.

sharedCredentials.introCopy: Use one browser session for all Zoho apps. Validating here uses Zoho CRM to confirm the session; when it succeeds, you can export from CRM, Books, and other apps you select.

fields.label: Organization ID
fields.userVisibleDescription: Organization identifier from your Zoho session headers.

fields.label: CSRF token
fields.userVisibleDescription: CSRF token from your Zoho session headers.

fields.label: Cookie
fields.userVisibleDescription: Full Cookie header value from the same logged-in browser session.

actions.label: Validate session
actions.behavior: Backend runs the CRM session smoke test with the pasted credentials.

canonicalCheck.userVisibleDescription: Validation uses Zoho CRM to confirm your session. When it succeeds, we trust it for your other Zoho apps as well.

onSuccess.userMessage: Session OK — you can export from the apps and data types you select.

onFailure.userMessage: Session could not be verified. Check your headers and that you are logged into Zoho CRM with the same browser session.

rules: User may select any combination of jobs across systems.
Only jobs with implementation === "available" participate in Download.
Jobs with implementation === "waitlist" use waitlist actions only; they do not produce export bytes until available.
Export produces at most one ZIP per system that has at least one selected available job; that ZIP contains outputs for all selected jobs under that system.

globalControls.label: Select all available
globalControls.behavior: Checks every job where implementation === "available" across all systems.

waitlistBehavior.secondaryCopy: Get notified when Workflows export is ready.
Get notified when Books Functions export is ready.
Get notified when Books Workflows export is ready.

primaryAction.label: Download
dependsOn: At least one selected job with implementation === "available"

behavior.aggregation: For each system with one or more selected available jobs, run the corresponding backend exports, then pack into one ZIP for that system.

zipNaming.note: Final filename patterns are user-customizable on the Settings page; main screen may show a read-only preview.

sections.title: ZIP file names
sections.description: Customize how downloaded ZIP files are named per system. Supported tokens (e.g. {{date}}, {{displayName}}) are defined by the implementation.

persistence.secretsPolicy: Never store Cookie, CSRF token, or raw session material in settings; only naming patterns and non-sensitive preferences.

developerRegistry.description: Internal mapping from backendJobId to scripts and HTTP details; not exposed in the UI.

allowed: Product display names (e.g. Zoho CRM, Zoho Books)
forbidden: Internal API URLs unless an explicit advanced or debug mode is added later

security.clientStorage: Do not persist session secrets in localStorage by default.

accessibility.checkboxGroups: Use fieldset/legend per system; support indeterminate state on parent when partially selected.
